ead.archdesc:
======= RMM03441Zonta Club (Ithaca, N.Y.) records3441Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
April 20112B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:

This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Zonta Club (Ithaca, N.Y.) records
Zonta Club (Ithaca, N.Y.)
3441
3.5 cubic feet. 3.5 cubic feet.
1919-1972. Correspondence, Financial Records, Manuscripts, Printed Materials Files relating to the Zonta club Collection material in English 2069121
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
April 2011
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, April 2011
Date modified:
Margaret Nichols, May 2016
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Zonta Club is a business and professional women's organization "to honor and encourage high ethical standards in business and professions."
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes bylaws; minutes of meetings (October 1919-December 1965); membership and committee rosters; committee reports; financial records (1919-1950); official correspondence (1920-1965); scrapbooks (2 volumes, 1932-1937); a file on Dr. Esther Parker, the founding president of the Ithaca club and the second International President; letters to Mrs. Leeta Houghton, an active member from ca.1945-1970, from a number of Hungarian families which the local club had adopted under the "Adopt-A-Family" Plan, Inc., concerning their family affairs, finances, and health, and ackowledging the receipt of packages from the club; and songbooks, photographs, historical data, and miscellaneous printed material.
Also, scattered and incomplete records of the regional organization (1932-1951), including printed and mimeographed district conference minutes, reports, and programs of Zonta International (1924-1963), including constitutions, articles of association, and bylaws; annual convention programs and proceedings; committee reports; a manual of procedure; membership classification guides; financial statements; a file on the Amelia Earhart Memorial Fund for scholarships; and copies of Zonta periodicals.
Cite As:
Zonta Club (Ithaca, N.Y.) records, #3441. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Songbooks. Scrapbooks. Photographs. Constitutions. Bylaws (administrative records) Women -- Societies and clubs. Houghton, Leeta. Parker, Esther, Dr. Zonta International
